methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $500M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Wandelbots, a Series G robotics and manufacturing software company that has raised $126M to date, operates in a sector with limited direct comparables. The peer universe for robotics and manufacturing includes one public company and eight private firms, though Stage G-specific peers with sufficient valuation transparency are sparse. This constraint limits the applicability of conventional peer multiples to this analysis.

Given data availability, the Stage-Progression method provides the primary quantitative anchor. Using total capital raised ($126M) as a proxy baseline and applying a 1.25x multiplier typical for Series G advancement, the analysis yields an implied valuation of $625M. Sensitivity around this step-up multiplier—reflecting uncertainty in market conditions and company-specific momentum—produces a low estimate of $531M and a high estimate of $719M. A Peer-Median approach was not feasible; while 11 same-sector companies exist, fewer than three qualified as direct comparables on stage, valuation tier, and data availability, making sector-wide multiples unreliable for precision.

The triangulated range of $531M to $719M, centered on $625M, should be treated as indicative rather than definitive. Key uncertainties include the appropriate step-up multiplier for private robotics software at this stage, the company's revenue scale and growth trajectory (not disclosed here), and broader market appetite for manufacturing automation plays. Additional disclosure on customer concentration, ARR, or recent fundraising terms would refine this estimate materially.
